inlineContent.text: To create a throwing task group,
call the
method.
Don’t use a task group from outside the task where you created it.
In most cases,
the Swift type system prevents a task group from escaping like that
because adding a child task to a task group is a mutating operation,
and mutation operations can’t be performed
from concurrent execution contexts like a child task.
Refer to
documentation for detailed discussion of semantics shared between all task groups.
A task group becomes canceled in one of the following ways:
when
is invoked on it,
when an error is thrown out of the
closure,
when the
running this task group is canceled.
Since a
is a structured concurrency primitive, cancellation is
automatically propagated through all of its child-tasks (and their child
tasks).
A canceled task group can still keep adding tasks, however they will start
being immediately canceled, and may act accordingly to this. To avoid adding
new tasks to an already canceled task group, use
rather than the plain
which adds tasks unconditionally.
For information about the language-level concurrency model that
is part of,
see
in

contexts without introducing a risk of data races.
